commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: But Peter and John stopped their mouths, by asking them, if it was reasonable for them to hearken to men rather than to God. For we, say they, (ver. 20.) cannot but speak the things which we have seen and heard. (Witham)

commentaries.author: Interlinear Gloss
commentaries.content:
Here by “God” they mean Christ, for he it was that commanded them.
Before He spoke or did any thing, Christ called Apostles, that neither word nor deed of His should be hid from their knowledge.
